locked_parameters.observable_distribution: P=P_{I,A,Y;\pi,\mathcal L,\mathcal C,\bar d,K} with finite n, known randomized design \pi, Y_I\in[0,1], observed network \mathcal L, candidate hidden edges \mathcal C, hidden-degree caps \bar d, and K\ge2.
locked_parameters.parameter_theta: \theta_{\mathrm{DI}}\in\mathbb R^{K+K(K-1)}, stacking totalized label-normalized direct contrasts D_k and ordered indirect contrasts I_{k\ell}.
locked_parameters.identifying_restrictions: \mathcal R_{\mathrm{sparse}\ K\mathrm{-support}}: finite population, known randomized assignment, bounded outcomes, consistency, sparse-network compatibility, exposure sufficiency through (A_i,e_i(A_{-i})), local positivity for active labels, deterministic label normalization, and |\operatorname{im}(e_i)|\le K.
locked_parameters.bounding_functional: \mathsf C_K(P)=\{x:u^\transpose x\le h_K(u;P)\text{ for all }u\}; sharp for the closed convex identified set \Theta_I(P), outer convex envelope for \Theta_I^{\mathrm{raw}}(P).

named_assumptions.label: ass:finite
named_assumptions.statement: The population size n, design \pi, observed network \mathcal L, candidate hidden-edge set \mathcal C, hidden-degree caps \bar d, and support bound K\ge2 are fixed and known. The sampled unit I is uniform on \{1,\ldots,n\}, the treatment vector satisfies A\in\{0,1\}^n, and Y_I\in[0,1].

named_assumptions.label: ass:sparse
named_assumptions.statement: The true interference graph belongs to \mathfrak G(\mathcal L,\mathcal C,\bar d): it contains \mathcal L, uses only hidden edges from \mathcal C, and has at most \bar d_i hidden edges incident to each unit i.

named_assumptions.label: ass:k-support
named_assumptions.statement: For each unit i, there exists an exposure map e_i:\{0,1\}^{n-1}\to\{0,\ldots,K-1\} that depends only on treatments of i's neighbors in the true graph and has image size at most K. Potential outcomes depend on other units' assignments only through e_i(A_{-i}).

named_assumptions.label: ass:labels
named_assumptions.statement: Exposure labels are normalized by the deterministic rule from the formal setup: sort labels first by \rho_{ik}(G,e), then by the conditional mean vector, and finally by lexicographic label name. All direct and indirect effects use these normalized labels.

named_assumptions.label: ass:consistency
named_assumptions.statement: For the true sparse exposure system (G,e), Y_i=Y_i(A_i,\ell_i^{G,e}(A_{-i})) and 0\le Y_i(a,k)\le1 for every unit i, own treatment a\in\{0,1\}, and normalized label k\in\{0,\ldots,K-1\}.

named_assumptions.label: ass:matching
named_assumptions.statement: A feasible completion (G,e,Y(\cdot)) must reproduce the observed conditional law of Y_I given (I,A) under the known assignment law \pi. Equivalently, every conditional mean and mass point of P_{I,A,Y} used by the finite support-function program is matched by the induced distribution from (G,e,Y(\cdot)).

named_assumptions.label: ass:positivity
named_assumptions.statement: For every active component of the mask M(G,e), the design \pi assigns positive probability to the relevant own-treatment and normalized-exposure events. Inactive direct labels with N_k=0 and inactive ordered labels with N_{k\ell}=0 remain in the fixed vector \theta_{\mathrm{DI}} by the zero-fill convention.

named_assumptions.label: ass:cellwise
named_assumptions.statement: The relaxation \mathcal F_{\mathrm{cell},K}(P) is evaluated with the same observed law P, bounded-outcome restrictions, totalized label coordinates, and local-positivity convention as \mathcal F_K(P), but its exposure-cell indicators need only satisfy per-unit design-propensity and support-size constraints. They need not be jointly generated by one sparse graph or one exposure map.

named_assumptions.label: ass:nondegenerate
named_assumptions.statement: For generic-class claims, the observed law belongs to an open class in which all treatment probabilities used by \pi, all reported label propensities, and all outcome cell means used by the support program are bounded away from 0 and 1. The open-set radius is measured in the finite-dimensional Euclidean topology on these probabilities and means.

named_assumptions.label: ass:audit
named_assumptions.statement: The finite direction class \mathcal U_K contains all coordinate directions, all direct-minus-indirect contrast directions, their negatives, and all normal directions needed for the finite witness family. The rectangle support h_{\mathrm{rect},K} optimizes each component over the same feasible class \mathcal F_K(P) before taking the rectangular product.

named_assumptions.label: ass:no-indirect
named_assumptions.statement: For the no-indirect sanity check only, every feasible response surface satisfies Y_i(a,k)=Y_i(a,\ell) for all units i, own treatments a, and normalized labels k,\ell.

estimand_functional: h_K(u;P)=\sup_{(G,e,Y)\in\mathcal F_K(P)}u^\transpose\theta_{\mathrm{DI}}(G,e,Y), with \mathsf C_K(P)=\{x:u^\transpose x\le h_K(u;P)\text{ for every }u\}.

conjectures.label: strict-cellwise-frontier
conjectures.statement:
(NOT YET PROVED.)  Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:finite}--\ref{ass:nondegenerate} and Assumption~\ref{ass:cellwise}, there is a nonempty open set \(\mathcal O_{\Gamma,K}\) of nondegenerate finite observed laws and a finite direction family \(\mathcal V_K\subset\mathbb R^{K+K(K-1)}\) such that, for every \(P\in\mathcal O_{\Gamma,K}\), some \(u\in\mathcal V_K\) satisfies
\[
\Gamma_K(u;P)=h_{\mathrm{cell},K}(u;P)-h_K(u;P)>0.
\]
For the \(K=2,n=4\) witness law \(P^\star\) in Corollary~\ref{cor:witness}, the conjectured lower bound is
\[
\max_{u\in\mathcal V_2}\Gamma_2(u;P^\star)\ge \frac{1}{40}.
\]
The focal mathematical object is the shared-map compatibility gap \(\Gamma_K\), which compares two independently defined support programs: the sparse graph-compatible program \(h_K\) and the cellwise relaxation \(h_{\mathrm{cell},K}\).

conjectures.label: joint-nonrectangular
conjectures.statement:
(NOT YET PROVED.)  Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:finite}--\ref{ass:audit}, there is an open set \(\mathcal O_K\) of finite observed laws \(P\) satisfying Assumption~\ref{ass:nondegenerate} such that
\[
\Delta_K(P)>0.
\]
For every \(P\in\mathcal O_K\),
\[
\Theta_I(P)\subsetneq
\prod_j [\inf_{\theta\in\Theta_I(P)}\theta_j,\ \sup_{\theta\in\Theta_I(P)}\theta_j],
\]
and there exists a direction \(u\in\mathcal U_K\) for which the componentwise rectangle overstates the sharp support value by exactly the direct-indirect coupling defect:
\[
h_{\mathrm{rect},K}(u;P)-h_K(u;P)=\Delta_K(P).
\]
For the concrete witness law \(P^\star\) in Corollary~\ref{cor:witness}, the conjectured certificate direction is \(u^\star=D_1-I_{01}\) and the conjectured margin is \(\Delta_2(P^\star)\ge1/40\).

conjectures.label: certificate
conjectures.statement: (NOT YET PROVED.)  Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:finite}--\ref{ass:audit}, for every finite direction \(u\in\mathcal U_K\) and endpoint \(t>h_K(u;P)\), endpoint rejection admits a finite certificate of exactly one of the following two types.  If \(t\notin\mathcal E_{\mathrm{cell}}(u,P)\), then \(t\) is separated by a violated bounded-response, observed-law, or Aumann-support inequality for the cellwise random set of feasible contrast selections.  If \(t\in\mathcal E_{\mathrm{cell}}(u,P)\), then the rejection has a sparse-network incompatibility certificate for the \(K\)-label exposure assignments, expressed through hidden-edge incidence, degree-cap, and label-support constraints.  In the special case \(K=2\) with binary ``unexposed/exposed'' labels and cellwise-feasible demanded exposure events, the network part reduces to a capacitated set-cover / b-matching feasibility certificate.

witness_law.label: cor:witness
witness_law.description: K=2,n=4 Bernoulli(1/2)^4 design, empty observed network, hidden candidates {{1,2},{2,3},{3,4}}, caps (1,2,2,1), path-neighbor exposure indicator, and Bernoulli outcomes with mean 0.30+0.16a+0.11z_L+0.07z_R+0.05a max{z_L,z_R}+eta_i for eta=(0.00,0.03,0.05,0.02).

substrate_survey.method: symbol search only; no source files loaded
substrate_survey.bypass_justified: The note is a finite combinatorial random-set envelope over latent network exposure maps; surveyed Manski and potential-outcome primitives are at a different abstraction and do not represent shared sparse graph/map compatibility.
